blocks.text: Cindergrove Advisors Inc is an operations consulting firm founded in 1995. We serve regional manufacturers and distributors who want practical improvement in the way their plants and warehouses run. The firm is small by design: five consultants, each of whom stays with the client from the first interview through the closing report. We do not sell studies that sit on a shelf. Every engagement ends with a written plan the client's own people can carry out, and with plain numbers to show whether it is working.

Our method is straightforward. We begin on the floor, not in the conference room. We count what can be counted, talk with the people who do the work, and put our findings in writing before we recommend anything. Proposals state the scope, the schedule, and the fee, and we hold to all three. The firm is led by its President, Carlos Griffith, who reads every proposal and every final report before it leaves the office. Clients deal with the consultants who do the work; there is no second team.

A representative engagement was the firm's work for {{fact:f:E-1998-001.client}}, where we reviewed production scheduling and work-in-process inventory on the plant floor. Our recommendations were adopted by plant management and carried out with the firm's assistance, and the results were reported to the client in writing at the close of the engagement. As with all of our client work, the particulars remain confidential. References are available to serious inquiries on request.

We would be pleased to discuss whether the firm's experience fits a prospective client's situation. Inquiries may be directed to the office in writing or by telephone, and they will receive a prompt and candid answer.
